realVeganQuestions: Do you actively avoid all animal products in your diet?
Do you check ingredient lists for hidden animal-based ingredients (e.g., gelatin, casein, carmine)?
Do you avoid honey and products containing bee-derived ingredients?
Have you researched which food additives (E-numbers) are not vegan?
Do you avoid buying products that contain palm oil due to deforestation risks?
Do you eat whole plant foods regularly (not just processed vegan junk food)?
Do you ensure your alcohol is vegan (no isinglass, gelatin, or egg whites used in filtration)?
Do you cook your own plant-based meals at home regularly?
Do you check if restaurant dishes are actually vegan (not just 'vegetarian')?
Do you support local farmers who grow organic, plant-based produce?
Do you avoid non-vegan supplements like fish oil or collagen?
Have you replaced cow's milk with a plant-based alternative in your coffee or tea?
Have you checked if your favorite snack brands are actually vegan?
Do you check if your personal care products (shampoo, toothpaste, makeup) are cruelty-free and vegan?
Do you avoid purchasing leather, suede, wool, or silk products?
Do you avoid down-filled jackets, pillows, and blankets?
Have you replaced conventional candles with soy or coconut wax alternatives?
Do you buy clothes made from sustainable, plant-based fabrics like organic cotton or hemp?
Do you choose cruelty-free household cleaning products?
Have you stopped buying cosmetics from brands that test on animals?
Do you avoid car interiors that contain leather?
Do you take plant-based omega-3s (algae-based) instead of fish oil?
Do you check if your vitamins and supplements are vegan?
Have you researched vegan-friendly medications and alternatives?
Do you make an effort to support vegan and cruelty-free pharmaceutical brands?
Do you try to prevent common deficiencies like B12, iron, and omega-3 with plant-based sources?
Do you reduce plastic waste by using reusable bags, bottles, and containers?
Do you compost food scraps instead of throwing them in the trash?
Do you avoid single-use plastics in your daily life?
Do you choose sustainable plant-based foods over heavily packaged products?
Do you actively educate yourself about deforestation caused by animal agriculture?
Do you consider the carbon footprint of imported plant foods?
Do you avoid brands that engage in greenwashing while still exploiting animals?
Do you shop at ethical, sustainable, and plant-based brands when possible?
Do you avoid brands that have been linked to deforestation or animal cruelty?
Do you support local, small-scale, and vegan businesses?
Have you tried to buy secondhand or thrifted clothes instead of fast fashion?
Do you try to reduce waste by buying in bulk or choosing minimal-packaging products?
Do you bring your own reusable bags to the grocery store?
Do you have conversations with friends and family about why you are vegan?
Do you advocate for plant-based alternatives in your workplace or school?
Have you attended any animal rights protests, demonstrations, or events?
Do you follow vegan news, documentaries, or activists to stay informed?
Do you share vegan content or recipes on social media?
Do you encourage friends or family to try plant-based meals?
Have you helped someone transition to a plant-based diet?
Do you support charities or organizations dedicated to animal rights and environmental causes?
Do you research vegan-friendly restaurants before traveling?
Have you successfully navigated non-vegan environments while staying plant-based?
Do you bring your own plant-based snacks when traveling to avoid non-vegan food options?
Do you use vegan-friendly travel booking services (like hotels that offer plant-based options)?
Have you chosen not to visit attractions that exploit animals (e.g., zoos, aquariums, elephant rides)?
Do you avoid companies that test on animals when shopping for technology (phones, laptops, etc.)?
Have you actively reduced or eliminated dairy from your diet?
Do you believe that all animals deserve to live free from harm?
Do you avoid attending events that exploit animals (e.g., rodeos, circuses, dog races)?
Have you helped someone understand the link between animal agriculture and climate change?
Do you make an effort to educate yourself on animal rights and ethical consumerism?
Have you ever knowingly eaten something non-vegan and justified it?
Do you ever buy 'accidentally vegan' products but ignore the ethics of the company?
Do you check food packaging for hidden dairy or egg ingredients?
Do you avoid companies that sell both vegan and non-vegan products?
Do you know the difference between plant-based and vegan?
Do you put effort into reducing harm even if perfection isn't possible?
Have you accepted that being 'imperfectly vegan' is still making a difference?
satiricalQuestions: Have you ever craved cheese?
Did you check if your toothpaste is cruelty-free?
Are your shoes actually vegan?
Have you ever killed a mosquito?
Did you eat a vegan burger from a store that sells meat?
Have you ever been to a zoo?
Did you ever walk past a butcher shop and not cry?
Have you ever not corrected someone who called oat milk 'milk'?
Did you buy that plant-based burger at a supermarket that also sells meat?
Have you ever eaten something without checking the label?
Have you ever shared a kitchen with a non-vegan?
Did your vegan bread get toasted in the same toaster as a non-vegan one?
Did you buy vegetables from a store that also sells dairy?
Have you ever forgotten to check if a product contains honey?
Do you eat bread? (Some brands use animal-derived L-cysteine!)
Have you ever used white sugar? (Some brands use bone char!)
Did you eat that vegan meal on an airplane with leather seats?
Have you ever craved an egg-based dessert?
Is your toothpaste 100% vegan and cruelty-free?
Did you check if your medications were tested on animals?
Are your shoes actually vegan, or just 'leather-free'?
Did you buy secondhand leather? (Doesn't matter, still leather!)
Are your tattoo inks vegan?
Do you wear wool sweaters inherited from your grandma?
Do you have old leather items you refuse to throw away?
Have you ever used a beauty product with beeswax?
Are your candles made from soy wax or paraffin? (Paraffin isn't vegan-friendly either!)
Did you ever sit on a leather couch and not scream in agony?
Did you take medicine knowing it was tested on animals?
Have you ever gotten vaccinated? (Most vaccines are tested on animals!)
Do you eat fortified foods that were tested on animals in clinical trials?
Did you check if your supplements were 100% plant-based?
Do you buy vegan protein powder, but in a plastic container?
Have you ever drunk tap water without verifying if the filtration process harmed animals?
Did you get a surgery in a hospital that serves non-vegan food?
Have you ever taken antibiotics? (They were tested on animals!)
Have you ever worn a band-aid without checking its ingredients?
Did you take public transport today? (Leather seats, fossil fuels, animal impact!)
Did you buy a product with palm oil?
Did you ever buy something made in a factory that pollutes the environment?
Have you ever driven a car with leather seats?
Is your phone case made from plastic? (Microplastics harm marine life!)
Have you ever thrown food away?
Did you order a vegan meal from a restaurant that also sells steak?
Have you ever ignored climate change while focusing on being vegan?
Did you ever stay silent when someone made a non-vegan joke?
Have you ever let someone eat meat near you?
Have you ever not corrected someone who called plant-based milk 'milk'?
Did you ever thank someone for cooking you a vegan meal even though they eat meat?
Have you ever dated a non-vegan?
Did you ever hug someone wearing a leather jacket?
Have you ever let a non-vegan borrow your kitchen utensils?
Did you ever not try to convert someone to veganism?
Have you ever called yourself 'plant-based' instead of 'vegan'?
Have you ever failed to educate someone when they asked why you're vegan?
Did you ever forget the exact date you went vegan?
Have you ever not read the full ingredients list on a product?
Have you ever failed to mention factory farming in a casual conversation?
Did you ever listen to a song without checking if the artist is vegan?
Did you ever watch a movie without researching if the actors are vegan?
Do you own books with glue that contains animal byproducts?
Have you ever killed a mosquito?
Have you ever owned a pet and not fed them a vegan diet?
Have you ever ridden a horse?
Have you ever been to a zoo?
Did you ever walk on grass without thinking of the insects below?
Did you ever visit a city with a history of hunting culture?
Do you drink water knowing microscopic organisms are inside?
Have you ever dreamed about eating cheese?
Did you ever watch a movie where someone ate meat and not turn it off?
Have you ever touched a leather seat without gloves?
Did you ever eat in a restaurant where meat was cooked in the same building?
Have you ever gone to a museum with taxidermy animals?
Have you ever smelled meat and thought it smelled good?
Did you ever admire a leather-bound book?
Have you ever walked past a butcher shop and not cried?
Have you ever forgotten to say 'Go vegan' at the end of a conversation?
Do you breathe oxygen that was once exhaled by non-vegans?
Have you ever eaten a vegan burger that was cooked on the same grill as meat?
Did you ever use a non-vegan's Wi-Fi?
Have you ever spoken to a non-vegan without handing them a pamphlet?
Have you ever been outside? (Insects everywhere!)
Have you ever existed in a non-vegan society?
Have you ever walked past a grocery store that sells eggs?
Have you ever bought anything, ever?
Did you blink while standing next to a non-vegan?
Are you sure you're vegan? Are you really sure? Are you 100% sure??

reflections.title: What is Vegan?
paragraphs: Language is both a liberation and a cage. The impossible standard of being "perfectly vegan" is unrealistic and unreachable because the threshold is set impossibly high.
This test was meant to expose the absurdity of binary thinking—framing veganism as either "pure" or "failed." But reality is more complex:
✅ If veganism is seen as an ethical identity, some would argue that even 99% is not enough.
✅ If veganism is seen as a directional commitment, then even 90% is meaningful progress.
✅ If veganism is a tool for change, then inclusivity matters more than perfection.
Purity is rarely the goal—intention and execution matter more than flawless adherence to a rule.

Veganism, viewed artistically, is not an untouched white canvas—it is a work-in-progress, layered with attempts, errors, adjustments, and evolution. A true philosophy of ethics should be invitational, not exclusionary.
We must ask: Is it more important to uphold the label or to increase the number of people moving toward ethical consumption?
No one is "perfectly ethical" in every choice. What matters is the effort to reduce harm, to do better, and to encourage others—not through judgment, but through inspiration.
